channel.title: The Brussels Times
channel.description: Latest Brussels posts from The Brussels Times.

item.title: 'Bootcamp for rioters'? Defence Minister wants army training after Brussels protests
item.description: While Theo Francken has condemned student protesters for their behaviour last week, the police have also come under scrutiny for allegedly heavy-handed tactics.

item.title: Brussels Airport passengers face four-hour queues due to EU border control rules
item.description: This is not the first time non-EU passengers have faced hours-long queues at passport control in Brussels Airport.

item.title: The most and least expensive areas to buy an apartment in Brussels
item.description: In Brussels, apartment prices can vary sharply from one municipality to another.

item.title: Fare dodgers beware: STIB clampdown in Brussels leads to €7m collected in fines
item.description: More passengers on Brussels public transport were subjected to a ticket inspection than the city's official population in 2025.

item.title: No more 'institutional lasagna' as Brussels starts updating its administration
item.description: The Brussels Government has approved the "first and most important" part of the upcoming reform, aiming to cut expenses.

item.title: Saint-Gilles urged to 'permanently pedestrianise' school street near busy Barrière
item.description: An open letter to Saint-Gilles mayor Jean Spinette (PS) is asking for the creation of a "garden street" in front of the school.

item.title: 'Saviours of the Aegidium': Forgotten Brussels palace rescued by Georgian dissident
item.description: The large palace-like hall was a symbol of Brussels' roaring twenties, but has been left abandoned for over 40 years. That soon may change.

item.title: Two Brussels rivers set to see the light of day after decades underground
item.description: For much of the 20th century, many of Brussels' streams and rivers were channelled underground to make way for urban development.

item.title: Brussels wildlife rescue centre plans to build €6 million animal hospital
The 'word of mouth' club bringing Brussels' Bulgarian community together
item.description: Around 50,000 Bulgarians live in Belgium, with the majority concentrated in Brussels and Flanders.

item.title: Brussels police present at Central Station amid protests
item.description: Police presence increased near Brussels Central Station after online calls for protests spark fears of further unrest, following days of violent demonstrations over Francophone education reforms.

item.title: Major fire in Anderlecht now under control
item.description: A warehouse fire in Anderlecht sent thick black smoke across Brussels, forcing evacuations and urgent safety warnings. Authorities remain on site to prevent reignition.

item.title: 'It's not if, it's when': Brits walk from London to Brussels to urge UK to rejoin EU
item.description: According to recent polls, 56% of British people support rejoining the EU, rising to 83% for 16- to 24-year-olds.

item.title: What to do in Brussels this weekend: 5 - 7 June
item.description: From a one-of-a-kind art fair taking over a hotel to an immersive electronic show, there is no shortage of ways to spend the first full weekend of June.

item.title: 'Shame' versus 'necessary': Education cuts in French-speaking Belgium divide politicians
item.description: The austerity measures in the French-speaking education sector have been passed against a backdrop of sustained social unrest and protests in Brussels.

item.title: Michelin-starred restaurant leaves Anderlecht for Brussels city centre
item.description: The restaurant, which has two Michelin stars, first opened in 1892.

item.title: Man suspected of firing AK-47 in Brussels metro on the run after breaking ankle tag
item.description: A stray bullet fired from the assault rifle landed in a child's bedroom on the ground floor of a house on Place Clémenceau.

item.title: Massive police deployments in Brussels as students continue to protest austerity measures
item.description: Students are protesting against austerity measures in French-speaking education.

item.title: Brussels Government approves first major administrative reform
item.description: "For years, we have talked about more cooperation, less fragmentation, and a more efficient administration. Today, we are turning those words into action."

item.title: Molotov cocktail thrown at home in Anderlecht
item.description: Around 04:00, police were called out to a fire at the front door of a residence on Rue des Mégissiers in Anderlecht.
